indexes.name: idx_lineitem_orderkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Repeated joins and EXISTS probes use lineitem via l_orderkey: Q3, Q4, Q9, Q11, Q17, Q20, and Q22 all join or semi-join lineitem to orders on l_orderkey = o_orderkey; Q17 also groups by l_orderkey in a subquery.
indexes.benefits: Reduces full scans of lineitem for order-to-lineitem joins, improves semi-join/EXISTS lookups, and helps grouped access by order key. High-confidence because lineitem is the largest table and this join key is used across many queries.
indexes.tradeoffs: Adds write and maintenance overhead on the largest table and consumes extra storage. Some queries may still prefer sequential scans when predicates are very unselective.

indexes.name: idx_orders_custkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Orders is repeatedly joined from customer by o_custkey in Q3, Q4, Q6, Q7, Q9, Q12, Q17, and checked in NOT EXISTS by customer key in Q21.
indexes.benefits: Speeds customer-to-orders joins and anti-joins, especially when customer-side filters first reduce the customer set, such as c_mktsegment in Q3 and phone/account filters in Q21.
indexes.tradeoffs: Extra index maintenance on inserts/updates to orders and additional storage. Existing o_orderdate index already supports date filters, so this adds another access path rather than replacing one.

indexes.name: idx_partsupp_partkey_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Q2, Q8, Q10, Q15, and Q19 repeatedly access partsupp by part key and then supplier key, or join on both keys together; Q8 specifically matches ps_partkey = l_partkey and ps_suppkey = l_suppkey.
indexes.benefits: Improves two-column joins against partsupp, supports correlated lookups by part, and is especially useful for Q8 and Q19 where both keys are involved. Higher value than separate single-column indexes because the workload frequently uses the pair.
indexes.tradeoffs: Consumes more storage than a single-column index and adds maintenance overhead on partsupp writes. Less useful for predicates on ps_suppkey alone due to leading-column order.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_partkey_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Q8 joins lineitem to partsupp on the pair (l_partkey, l_suppkey), and Q19 correlates lineitem by both part and supplier under a shipdate range. This pair appears together in the same workload patterns more than once.
indexes.benefits: Supports pairwise joins and correlated lookups involving part-supplier combinations, reducing lineitem scan cost in queries that otherwise must filter a large fact table using two dimensions.
indexes.tradeoffs: Additional maintenance overhead on lineitem and possible overlap with other lineitem indexes. Limited benefit for queries using only l_suppkey or only l_partkey.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_receiptdate_partial
indexes.workload_pattern: Q11 and Q22 both use the predicate l_commitdate < l_receiptdate; Q11 also filters a receipt-date range, and Q20 uses late-receipt logic in correlated checks.
indexes.benefits: Targets only late-receipt rows, shrinking index size relative to a full index and accelerating recurring late-shipment queries. The indexed receipt date supports the range predicate used with the same business condition.
indexes.tradeoffs: Useful mainly when the late-receipt predicate remains common; if most rows satisfy the predicate, selectivity advantage drops. Still adds maintenance cost for qualifying lineitem rows.

global_observations: The workload is TPC-H-like and read-heavy, with most benefit concentrated on join keys in lineitem, orders, and partsupp.
Very small dimension tables such as region, nation, and supplier have low row counts, so additional indexes there are unlikely to outperform sequential scans.
Existing single-column indexes already cover lineitem.l_shipdate, orders.o_orderdate, and several part attributes; suggestions avoid duplicating those exact paths.

Text predicates with leading wildcards or function-wrapped columns (for example LIKE '%...%' and LOWER(...)) are poor candidates for standard PostgreSQL B-tree indexes under the allowed actions, so they were not recommended.
Because lineitem is the largest table, only high-confidence indexes with repeated workload support were proposed to balance read gains against write and storage cost.
ontology_notes: RuleHypSimpleIndex supports missing single-column indexes on frequently used join/filter columns such as l_orderkey and o_custkey. RuleHypCompositeIndex supports paired-column recommendations where the same-table column combination appears together in workload predicates or joins, such as partsupp(ps_partkey, ps_suppkey) and lineitem(l_partkey, l_suppkey). RuleSimplePartialIndex supports a PostgreSQL partial index for the recurring predicate l_commitdate < l_receiptdate. A redundant composite suggestion on orders(o_orderdate, o_custkey) was removed because an index already exists on o_orderdate and the added composite path was not sufficiently justified against overlap.
